sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
New patient visit for acid reflux.
History of Present Illness

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value: I've just got really bad acid reflux. Though I mean I... right now, I take 40 milligrams of omeprazole twice a day. It feels like burning. Any difficulty with swallowing? Any abdominal pain? Nausea or vomiting, changes in bowel movements? And how long have you had this problem? I've had acid reflux since I was around 12, but it has gotten worse over the past few years. And how long have you been on the omeprazole? Are there any things that make your symptoms worse that you can pinpoint, like food choices or stress? Probably stress and food choices sometimes. We do know that caffeine and caffeinated beverages will do that, even sodas. It's reassuring that you're not having any difficulty swallowing, though. And any family history of GI issues? Um, my grandmother had esophageal cancer. And my mom had some issues like acid reflux, and she was taking some medication that coats the lining of your stomach. Have you ever tried any medication other than omeprazole? Um, at one point I was on omeprazole and ranitidine -dine. But other than that, those are the only medications I have taken. What's the surgical scar here on your abdomen from? My mom said my intestines were twisted, so I had to have surgery when I was around one month old. From what you've told me, it sounds like you've had a several-year history of Gerd, which is the medical term for acid reflux, and recently your symptoms have been poorly controlled with the omeprazole, 40 milligrams twice daily. So the first thing I'd try is changing your diet because these foods can exacerbate your Gerd, even though you're taking the ppi. Stress is another trigger that we need to address. High stress can cause worsening of your symptoms. Yeah, stress and anxiety are definitely big problems for me. So working on that may help with your Gerd. Additionally, we can consider trying a different ppi since the only one you've tried up to this point is omeprazole. If you're okay with this, we can switch your omeprazole to nexium. I will send a prescription for nexium 40 milligrams twice daily to your pharmacy on file.

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Findings
The patient has well-healed abdominal surgical scars noted. A normal physical exam was performed.
Assessment
The patient presents with a likely diagnosis of gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD).
Plan
I'll recommend that you have a procedure called an EGD, or upper endoscopy, to look for potential causes of the Gerd. We'd sedate you and then pass a small camera through your mouth, down your throat, through your stomach, and end in the small intestines. I'll have you follow up with me in the office for the EGD so we can discuss the results and talk about how you're doing with the dietary, stress, and medication changes. Be sure to call my office and let me know if anything changes or if you have problems before your EGD and follow-up appointment. I suspect that she likely has Gerd with underlying functional etiology as well. If her EGD is normal and her symptoms persist despite a trial of nexium, we can consider a trial of protonix and obtain a pH-impedance study. This will allow us to rule out abnormal acid reflux exposure and to decipher between a functional etiology and hypersensitivity.
